title: Zero stability review — worst offenders
subtitle: 17 Mar – 24 Jun 2026 (90 days) · Rocicorp · 123 real incidents
summary: Over the 90-day window the org filed 159 incidents; 123 were “real” (active / closed / post-incident) after dropping 36 triage-only records with no work. Volume is rising month over month (5 → 40 → 58 → 56), but this tracks customer / stack onboarding more than a reliability regression — the more important signal is how repetitive the failure mix is.

Stability pain is concentrated in the server-side sync path: replication-manager and view-syncer account for nearly every incident, all funnelled through a single alert source (cloudzero logs + amp). Severity is under-graded — only ~20% are Major+ and AI urgency was never assessed — so these should be triaged by content, not by severity field.

Four root-cause clusters dominate: (1) managed-Postgres failover / replication-slot loss forcing full Zero resyncs, (2) the replica failing to keep up with upstream write bursts, (3) fragile DDL / schema-change handling, and (4) recoverable conditions that crash the process or page on-call. IVM/ZQL engine bugs are fewer but are genuine product defects.

Most issues are mitigated or worked around, not root-fixed. Of the pivotal incidents reviewed, several recurred (slot-loss INC-331 → INC-556, missing PG event-triggers INC-485 → INC-487, ddl-ordering INC-143 repeated). Only 11% of follow-ups are closed and most Majors generated no follow-up at all — so recurrence is not being systematically prevented. The post-incident loop is the meta-finding.

charts.title: Incidents by root-cause theme (analyst categorisation, approx)

tables.title: Root-cause themes, ranked — status reflects deepest fix found, not severity
rows: Managed-PG failover / slot loss / auth / connection limits
Recoverable error → crash or false page

sections.title: Highest-leverage fix: survive managed-Postgres failover without a full resync
narrative: The single largest incident generator is Zero's dependence on customer-managed Postgres (PlanetScale / Supabase / Neon). When the upstream fails over, resizes, or resets, the replication slot is lost and Zero is forced into a full resync — producing replication-lag alerts, container restart loops, and real downtime (INC-331 was ~30 minutes).

This class is recurring and only mitigated. INC-331 was closed by manually protecting the slot, then the same failure recurred as INC-556 (a PlanetScale resize) which is still unresolved. Two follow-ups target the root cause but are open: changing slot-cleanup logic on interrupted initial sync (INC-351) and adding automatic subscription retry (INC-347).

Fixing this once — retain/recreate the slot across failover, retry subscription automatically, and avoid dropping the slot on transient interruption — would remove a large fraction of both incidents and out-of-hours pages.

incidents.summary: PlanetScale failover → slot loss → Zero reset, ~30m downtime (mitigated by manual slot protection)
PlanetScale resize triggered reset → resync; recurrence of the slot-loss class, still unresolved
Open follow-up: don't drop the slot when initial sync is interrupted, to avoid restart loops
Open follow-up (Major): RM did not auto-retry subscription after slot issue
Supabase connection reset → RM crash-looped on startup, ~2.5h lag

sections.title: Open right now — 16 outstanding follow-ups (avg 41 days old)
narrative: Across 123 incidents only 18 follow-ups were ever created and just 2 are closed. The 16 open items below are the entire tracked remediation backlog — the oldest has been open 90 days. 30 incidents also remain in a non-closed state (active / investigating / monitoring / fixing).

Note the quality gap: the recent follow-ups (INC-783, 793, 794) are exemplary — full root cause, PR links, design notes — while most Majors generated no follow-up at all. The process works when used; it just isn't used consistently.

rows.text: Document privilege/ownership setup for custom roles
Graceful handling for transient 'database is locked'
Add statement timeout to prevent replication deadlocks
Address recurring giant transactions causing lag
Verify SQL syntax fix is present in 1.4 release
Retry / graceful handling for Supavisor failures
Resolve deadlock during RM changeLog takeover
Fix missing auto-retry for RM subscription failures
Change slot cleanup logic on initial-sync interruption
Handle/retry storer write-statement timeouts vs crashing
Keep catchup snapshot alive vs idle-in-transaction kill
Root cause: replica can't keep up w/ upstream volume
Avoid full-table backfill for ADD COLUMN w/ enum DEFAULT
Prevent column backfill from blocking replication stream

sections.title: Theme — Managed-Postgres instability (~26 incidents)
narrative: Beyond failover/slot loss, this cluster includes password-auth failures (INC-393, 391, 388, 305, 12, 8), connection-refused / unreachable DB (INC-617, 474, 287), and superuser connection-slot exhaustion (INC-743, 600). Many are customer-config issues, but Zero amplifies them into crashes, restart loops, and pages rather than degrading gracefully.

incidents.summary: Connection limits — all slots reserved for superuser, view-syncer reaper can't connect
6 errors: Postgres password auth failures closing connections
Connection refused to DB host:5432, retrying every 10s

sections.title: Theme — Replica can't keep up with upstream write bursts
narrative: A distinct, recurring performance failure: large upstream transactions / write bursts build replication lag, the slot accumulates WAL until managed-Postgres caps or invalidates it, and recovery replaying the backlog overwhelms writes — looping back into lag. INC-793 captured backpressure hitting a ~184MB buffer cap with 300K+ queued changes. This root cause is explicitly the only open High-priority follow-up.
INC-43's follow-up notes two consecutive days of giant-transaction lag; INC-21 traced a stall to statement_timeout=0 allowing an indefinite deadlock.

incidents.summary: Margins/Supabase: slot exceeded max size + change-streamer crash storm, ~37m lag (High-pri root-cause follow-up open)
Hinoki: ADD COLUMN w/ enum default forced 384k-row backfill that blocked the stream
360s lag from a >1M-row transaction; second day running
Replicator stalled 16h: statement_timeout=0 allowed a back-pressure deadlock

sections.title: Theme — DDL & schema-change fragility (~16 incidents)
narrative: The change-streamer's handling of DDL is brittle and recurring. Failure modes: 'ddlUpdate received without ddlStart' halting replication (INC-143, 121); 'missing property event' TypeErrors (INC-166, 154, 153); new column arriving without DDL because PG event triggers weren't installed (INC-485, which recurred as INC-487); array/enum column SQL-syntax errors (INC-149, 148, 146, 140); and schema-change + backfill that crash-loops until an RM resync (INC-477). Most are mitigated by resync, not root-fixed.

incidents.summary: DDL table-recreation exposed an intermediate schema; backfill upsert crash-looped until RM resync (still Investigating)
New column w/o DDL — event triggers not installed; recurred as INC-487, no code fix
Replication halted: ddlUpdate without preceding ddlStart; noted as happened again
Array-column ALTER TABLE syntax error; follow-up to verify fix shipped is still open

sections.title: Theme — Recoverable errors that crash or false-page (~13 incidents)
narrative: A high-ROI cluster: recoverable or expected conditions surface as unhandled rejections / ERROR logs that crash a worker or page on-call. Examples: change-streamer write timeouts becoming unhandledRejection → exit 13 (INC-793); intentional resync logged as exit code 255 (INC-40, since fixed); Supavisor crashes paging unnecessarily (INC-239); bad connection string → unhandled exception (INC-209). A focused error-classification pass removes both incidents and noise at once.

incidents.summary: FIXED — intentional view-syncer shutdown was misclassified as a crash (exit 255)
Transient Supavisor crash paged on-call; two open follow-ups to suppress + handle gracefully
Cold-start restore self-timeout 29s before backup was ready; fixes #6134/#6135/#1082 in flight

sections.title: Theme — IVM / ZQL engine bugs (~10 incidents)
narrative: Fewer in number but the purest product-correctness signal — these are bugs in your own query engine, not the environment. Several are slated for or shipped in 1.6, but some root causes remain open behind error-trapping band-aids.

incidents.summary: FIXED (#5930, 1.6) — 'CVR concurrently modified' ProtocolError taking clients offline
Corrupt IVMBranch → 'row already exists' on reconnect; #5910/#5916 trap the symptom, root cause open
'UnionFanIn requires sorted input' closing connections (fix unconfirmed)
ZQL IVM Take operator bug terminated a client group's session

sections.title: Post-incident process gap
narrative: Only 18 follow-ups exist across 123 incidents, and just 2 are completed (11%). The mean open age is 41 days; the oldest is 90. Most Major incidents produced no follow-up, so the same failure classes recur without a tracked owner or fix. This is the meta-finding: the recurrence documented throughout this report is a direct consequence of an unused post-incident loop.

Recommendation in brief: require a follow-up on every Major, set priorities (15 of 16 open items have none), and burn down the existing backlog — several open items already have fixes in PRs and just need closing out.

sections.title: Known gaps — what this report does not tell you
narrative: Severity is under-graded (only ~20% Major+) and AI escalation-urgency was never assessed, so impact ranking relies on incident content, not the severity field.

Root-cause theme counts and per-stack counts are analyst categorisations / name-match approximations (marked ~), not exact query aggregates — incident.io name search tokenises loosely. Treat them as proportions, not precise totals.
Postmortems are sparse and follow-ups cover only ~15% of incidents, so 'fix status' is drawn from investigation findings and fix-language in summaries; where neither existed it is marked unconfirmed.
June is a partial month (through the 24th). The window is 90 days with no prior baseline, so month-over-month trend is directional, not seasonal.

recommendations.title: Make Zero survive managed-Postgres failover / slot loss without a full resync
recommendations.detail: Largest incident generator and the source of the worst downtime (INC-331 ~30m, INC-476 ~2.5h). Retain or auto-recreate the replication slot across failover/resize, auto-retry the subscription, and stop dropping the slot on transient initial-sync interruption. Two root-cause follow-ups (INC-351, INC-347) already exist but are open.

evidence.narrative: INC-331 was closed by manually protecting the slot, then recurred as INC-556 (resize) which is unresolved. The same coupling drives restart-loop and replication-lag alerts on Goblins (PlanetScale) and Margins (Supabase).

incidents.summary: Failover → slot loss → reset, ~30m
Resize → reset; recurrence, unresolved
Open: add auto-retry for subscription failures

recommendations.title: Fix replica throughput & backpressure under upstream write bursts
recommendations.detail: The replica can't sustain bursty upstream volume: lag builds, the slot caps/invalidates, recovery overwhelms writes, and it loops. This is the only open High-priority follow-up (INC-793). Investigate change-streamer write throughput vs upstream volume, buffer/slot sizing, and smoothing large transactions; make slow backfills stream incrementally instead of blocking the stream.

incidents.summary: Backpressure hit 184MB cap, 300K+ queued changes, crash storm
Enum-default ADD COLUMN forced a 384k-row backfill blocking the stream
>1M-row transaction, two days running

recommendations.title: Harden the DDL / schema-change replication path
recommendations.detail: Make constant/enum-default ADD COLUMN metadata-only (no full backfill), fix array-column SQL generation, and make ddlUpdate/ddlStart ordering and missing event-triggers self-healing rather than replication-halting. This cluster (~16) is largely mitigated-by-resync and recurring.

incidents.summary: Event triggers not installed; recurred as INC-487
ddlUpdate without ddlStart; repeated
Schema-change backfill crash-loop until resync

recommendations.title: Run an error-classification & alert-noise sweep
recommendations.detail: Audit which conditions log ERROR / throw unhandledRejection and crash or page. Downgrade recoverable/transient cases (Supavisor crashes, transient 'database is locked', intentional resync exits, slow-upstream write timeouts) to retry-or-warn. Removes incidents and out-of-hours pages simultaneously; INC-40 proves the pattern works.

incidents.summary: Two open follow-ups: suppress + gracefully handle Supavisor crashes
Open: graceful handling for transient 'database is locked'

recommendations.title: Close the post-incident loop
recommendations.detail: Only 11% of follow-ups are closed and most Majors have none. Require a follow-up on every Major, assign priorities (15 of 16 open items are unprioritised), and burn down the backlog — several open items already have fixes in PRs and just need closing.

evidence.narrative: 18 follow-ups total across 123 incidents; 2 completed; mean open age 41 days, oldest 90. The recurrence seen across every theme above is downstream of this.

recommendations.title: Burn down IVM / ZQL engine bugs and confirm 1.6 fixes landed
recommendations.detail: These are genuine engine-correctness defects (Take operator, UnionFanIn, IVMBranch corruption, CVR concurrent-modify). Confirm the 1.6 fixes (e.g. #5930) actually shipped and that band-aided cases (INC-200, #5910/#5916) get a real root-cause fix.

incidents.summary: Fixed in 1.6 (#5930)
Symptom trapped; root cause open
